description_long: This is a rounded, hand-crafted clay vase with a wide opening and a slightly irregular rim, showing evidence of age and use. The surface is adorned with a band of faded, reddish-brown geometric triangle motifs encircling the upper portion, suggestive of traditional or ancient decorative styles. The body of the vase is bulbous, tapering gently toward the base, and the texture appears rough and matte, with visible imperfections and natural variations in the clay. There are areas where the painted decoration has worn away, revealing the natural color of the fired clay beneath. The rim is uneven and chipped in places, indicating wear or possible damage over time. The interior is dark and unglazed, consistent with utilitarian pottery meant for holding water or other materials.
description: A rounded, hand-crafted clay vase with faded reddish geometric patterns and a rough, matte surface, showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the most intact geometric triangle motifs and a visible chip on the rim.
description_view_1: This side reveals a mostly plain surface with faint remnants of the pattern and a smoother, less decorated area.
description_view_2: The pattern is partially visible but more faded, with the surface showing subtle color variations and fewer decorations.
description_view_3: This view highlights the most worn and faded section of the pattern, with additional surface discoloration and rim chipping.
